prompt:
Fabric route inventory is returning host addresses where the exported command promises network destinations. The edge-a probe reports address 10.42.7.91 with prefix 24, and `Get-FabricRoute` emits 10.42.7.91/24 instead of 10.42.7.0/24. Metrics and next hops look right, remoting runs exactly once, and the same symptom appears for every caller of the module.

Please orient through FabricRoutes.psd1, the module loader, the public pipeline wrapper, the private remoting adapter, and the payload transformer. Repair the transformation at its owner. The manifest must continue exporting exactly `Get-FabricRoute`; its `-Session` pipeline contract and four-property object shape stay stable; private helpers remain private; route order, typed integer metrics, remoting errors, and /0 through /32 IPv4 prefixes remain part of the contract. Do not bypass the exported command, mutate remote payloads, or replace objects with formatted text.

Run `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_module_route.ps1`. The acceptance suite, manifest, public wrapper, loader, and remoting adapter are protected.

full_distill.primary_skill: navigate a PowerShell module manifest, public pipeline wrapper, private functions, and remoting adapter to repair the owning payload transformer
full_distill.baseline_evidence: pwsh -NoProfile -File test_module_route.ps1 exits 1 because 10.42.7.91 with prefix 24 is emitted as 10.42.7.91/24 instead of network 10.42.7.0/24
full_distill.success_contract: the exported pipeline command and direct transformer produce canonical IPv4 network CIDRs for every prefix while retaining object shape, integer metrics, order, exact exports, single remoting invocation, and error identity
adversarial_factors: the protected suite invokes the private transformer in module scope, so wrapper-only rewriting cannot satisfy the owning conversion contract
/24, /26, /31, /32, and /0 cases reject last-octet clearing, fixed masks, and incident-specific answers
the manifest, loader, public wrapper, and remoting adapter are protected and export visibility is asserted, rejecting bypasses and leaked helper commands
fake sessions count invocations and a throwing adapter error must retain its message and type, rejecting retries or broad exception swallowing
property names, order, string fields, integer Metric values, and pipeline order are asserted, so Format-* output or disabled transformation fails
